name: Pegasus Spyware
description: Pegasus is a highly sophisticated spyware developed by the Israeli cyber-arms company NSO Group. It is capable of infecting phones running iOS and Android operating systems, allowing operators to extract a wide range of data, including messages, emails, photos, call logs, and location data. It can also activate the phone's microphone and camera to record conversations and take pictures. The spyware's capabilities have raised significant concerns about privacy, security, and human rights. Proponents of the theory that Pegasus is misused by governments and other malicious actors point to leaked data and forensic analysis indicating its widespread use against journalists, human rights activists, politicians, and other individuals deemed threats. The evidence often involves technical reports detailing the unique digital signatures and methods of infection used by Pegasus. NSO Group maintains that it sells Pegasus only to vetted governments for the purpose of fighting crime and terrorism and that it investigates allegations of misuse. However, numerous reports from organizations like Amnesty International and Citizen Lab have detailed instances where Pegasus was used to target individuals who do not fit this description. Mainstream perspectives acknowledge the existence and capabilities of Pegasus but emphasize the complex legal and ethical considerations surrounding its use. They highlight the potential benefits of such technology in combating serious crime and terrorism while acknowledging the risks of abuse and the need for greater oversight and accountability. The debate centers on balancing national security interests with individual privacy rights and the potential for authoritarian governments to use such tools to suppress dissent.
subtitle: Controversial Israeli Spyware Technology

claims.claim: Pegasus is used to target journalists and human rights activists.
claims.evidence: Forensic analysis of phones belonging to journalists and activists showing Pegasus infection, leaked data from NSO Group.
claims.criticism: NSO Group claims it investigates misuse and terminates contracts when abuse is confirmed. They argue that the provided evidence is circumstantial or fabricated.

claims.claim: Pegasus is sold to authoritarian regimes for surveillance of dissidents.
claims.evidence: Reports from human rights organizations detailing Pegasus use in countries with poor human rights records, evidence of targeting political opponents.
claims.criticism: NSO Group states it has a rigorous vetting process and sells only to legitimate governments for fighting crime and terrorism. They claim they cannot control how clients ultimately use the software.

claims.claim: Pegasus can be deployed without any user interaction (zero-click exploit).
claims.evidence: Technical analysis of Pegasus's infection methods, including zero-click exploits targeting vulnerabilities in iOS and Android.
claims.criticism: NSO Group acknowledges the existence of zero-click exploits but claims they are necessary to bypass encryption and prevent criminals from evading law enforcement. They argue that they work to patch vulnerabilities as they are discovered.

keyFigures.name: Shalev Hulio
keyFigures.role: Co-founder and former CEO of NSO Group

keyFigures.name: Ron Deibert
keyFigures.role: Director of Citizen Lab, a research group that has extensively investigated Pegasus

timeline.event: First reports of Pegasus targeting UAE human rights activist
WhatsApp sues NSO Group for allegedly exploiting a vulnerability to install Pegasus on user devices
The Pegasus Project, a collaborative investigation by multiple media outlets, reveals widespread abuse of Pegasus spyware
The US Department of Commerce adds NSO Group to its Entity List, restricting exports to the company

sources.author: Amnesty International
sources.work: Forensic Methodology Report: How to catch NSO Group's Pegasus
sources.title: Amnesty International

sources.author: Scott Shane
sources.work: Private Companies Sell Spy Tools to Despots. The U.S. Is Silent.
sources.title: Scott Shane
